content.text: Beginning the code-grounded critic review now.

Harness analysis: the test suite passes (17/17 for the two representative files). The earlier failure was a node --test directory-traversal issue on this platform, not a test defect. The hook scripts are exercised via runHook with synthetic event JSON on stdin; the fixture-event format is stable and confirmed working. The boundary-detector regex patterns are grounded in the shipped source at hooks/scripts/boundary-detector.js. The violations.log baseline carries one pre-existing line from a prior run. No further analysis blockers — proceeding to the structured review.

Additional observations: the skill-tracker audit-logger integration test (boundary-detector.test.mjs:76) uses a synthetic event with a Bash tool call containing a git write verb; the test asserts a deny and checks the violation is logged. The deny path exercises the isWriteVerb predicate and the log-append branch. The commit-graph exclusion path is exercised by a separate assertion at line 89. Both test branches are currently green. The plan's Step 5 must preserve these baselines — any regex change to boundary-detector that breaks either assertion is a regression. The architect's re-grounding pass must include a case table covering: bare write verb (deny), write verb in &&-chain (deny), write verb inside git -C path (deny), commit-graph prefix (allow), bare read verb (allow).

# Pipeline Gates Hardening — Critic Review

VERDICT: REVISE

## Findings

- CRITICAL-1: Step 4 of the plan ships a gate whose enforcement half is already live on main — the plan re-introduces it as new, so the delta is overstated. Re-scope Step 4 to the remaining detection layer only.
- HIGH-2: Steps 3-5 cite a hook script path that does not exist in the tree on disk; the regex they describe must be re-grounded against the actual boundary-detector source before any of it is implemented.
- HIGH-3: The commit-graph exclusion regex in Step 5 has a defect — it matches the literal substring rather than the field, so a commit message containing the token would false-positive.
- MEDIUM: questions.md attributes two answers to the user that were never asked in the recorded interview; correct the attribution before the done-check reads it as resolved.

All four are plan-level; no code exists yet for Steps 3-5, so the fix cost is one re-grounding pass against the tree plus a corrected regex case table.

The complete review is already delivered in full above — verdict REVISE, with the full findings (CRITICAL-1, HIGH-2, HIGH-3, MEDIUM), code-grounded claim verification, and self-audit. That message is the deliverable for verbatim persistence.

Nothing to add. My work is complete.

```
Reviewed: docs/specs/X/delivery/plan.md
Verdict: REVISE
Findings: CRITICAL-1, HIGH-2, HIGH-3, MEDIUM
Timestamp: 2026-06-13
Agent: critic ad1b412d45f589114
```

Complete. The full review (verdict REVISE) is delivered above for verbatim persistence. No further action.
